description_long: This is a large commercial jet airplane with a streamlined fuselage painted primarily white, featuring green engine nacelles and a green tail assembly. The aircraft has four underwing engines, two on each wing, and a swept low-wing configuration with slight dihedral. The tail section includes a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, all colored green, with the vertical stabilizer displaying the text 'BASH' and a stylized logo. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage, with multiple windows visible along the side for passengers. The landing gear is visible beneath the wings and nose, indicating a tricycle configuration with retractable gear. The overall design suggests a modern, wide-body passenger jet suitable for long-haul flights.
description: A large, white commercial jet airplane with green engines and tail, featuring four underwing engines and a swept low-wing design. The fuselage displays passenger windows and a forward cockpit, with a conventional tail and tricycle landing gear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pointed nose, cockpit windows, and the full span of the swept wings with four visible engines and green tail surfaces.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the elongated fuselage, passenger windows, green underwing engines, and the green tail fin with 'BASH' branding.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the green horizontal and vertical stabilizers, the full wingspan, and the alignment of the engines under the wings.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the opposite side of the fuselage, engines, and tail, with clear visibility of the passenger windows and branding.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, swept configuration with slight dihedral and no visible winglets.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle retractable landing gear with two main gear units and one nose gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
